rulings.Case Title: KIMI NORWAY VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, August 23, 2024, Line 7. 1 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's ("SFUSD") motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And For Order Setting Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, August 23, 2024, Line 11. DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And For Order Setting Aside Default. Defendant Yan's motion to quash/vacate default is granted. Yan declares that Bryant Fu never served him with a copy of the summons and complaint. That declaration curiously fails to explain how Yan learned of this case or how "by chance" he discovered the purportedly false proof of service days after it was filed with the court on March 21, 2023. In opposition, plaintiff Fu cites the proof of service which shows that Yan was allegedly served on September 26, 2022, at the court. It is unclear why this proof of service was filed nearly six months later. Moreover, Bryant Fu does not provide a declaration amplifying the precise circumstances surrounding the alleged service. Based on this vague and disputed record, the court grants the motion to quash/vacate. "Because the law favors disposing of cases on their merits, 'any doubts in applying section 473 must be resolved in favor of the party seeking relief from default [citations].'" (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.) Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)
